title: Identical expressions should be avoided on both sides of a binary operator
description: Identical expressions should not be used on both sides of a binary operatorAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcertWhy is this an issue?More InfoUsing the same value on both sides of a binary operator is a code defect. In the case of logical operators, it is either a copy/paste error and, therefore, a bug, or it is simply duplicated code and should be simplified. In the case of most binary mathematical operators, having the same value on both sides of an operator yields predictable results and should be simplified as well.ExceptionsThe following are ignored:When an increment or decrement operator is used, ex:*p++ == *p++Bitwise operators|, &, ^, <<Arithmetic operators+, *Assignment operators=, +=, *=Code examplesNoncompliant code examplevoid foo(int a, int b) { if ( a == a
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Identical expressions should be avoided on both sides of a binary operator. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
Redundant comparison detected. This expression is always true or always false.
Identical expressions in arithmetic operation. Result is predictable and should be simplified.
